author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
Status update (2026-06-22): cutover landed.

git-bug â†’ jjforge cutover is complete. Commit `98f49d2`:
`docs/git-bug-cutover.md` (bridge doc with old â†’ new id
mapping + how to read archived `refs/bugs/*`) and a full
CLAUDE.md rewrite (jjforge-first, new roadmap entry-point,
queries swapped to `jjf ls`).

Follow-up cleanup commit `edbaced`: the op-space merge
round (`adfc716`) had accidentally removed
`experiments/jj-cli-overhead/test-repo/a.txt` from the
index via jj's snapshot machinery. Restored, plus a
narrow `.gitignore` rule for `experiments/**/test-repo/.jj/`
so the same footgun doesn't return.

This makes `cli-replace-shim` genuinely ready. Per the
subagent: the shim's planner role is done; flipping it
removes the footgun where a future `bin/jjf new` would
land in the wrong tracker. Small ticket â€” file it under
this epic and dispatch when you're ready.

Migration trail for archival lookups:
- Decision: `git-bug bug show d12031c` (closed, option B
  recorded).
- Cutover plan + execution: `git-bug bug show 176f3c6`
  (closed, includes the full oldâ†’new id table in the
  closing comment).
- Cutover doc: `docs/git-bug-cutover.md`.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
Status update (2026-06-22): colocate-drift bug closed.

- `08cf14b` â†’ closed. Commit `e8760b0`. New preflight
  `refuse_self_hosted_write` detects the jjforge source
  repo via marker-combo (`crates/jjf/Cargo.toml` +
  `docs/storage-format.md`) and refuses mutating verbs
  with kind `self_hosted_write_refused`. Bypass:
  `JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST=1` (loud in text mode, silent
  under `--json`). Reads pass through unmodified.

Tests: 185 workspace-wide (was 178). 7 new in
`crates/jjf/tests/self_host_guard.rs`.

CLAUDE.md "Operating in a colocated jj+git repo" section
rewritten with the corrected recovery recipe (`git
symbolic-ref HEAD refs/heads/main && git reset --hard
main` â€” the old `jj edit main` advice was empirically
incomplete).

Per the bugs-before-features rule (now codified as step
2 of the orchestration loop), this unblocks `agent-ready`
(`69d5e1b`) which was the next feature ticket. The
orchestration loop can now safely run `jjf` from inside
this repo when `JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST=1` is set.

Closed-not-filed follow-ups (subagent surfaced, none
blocking): option B (full workspace isolation) as a
separate ticket if the bypass becomes friction-heavy;
a single `jjforge.toml` marker if the two-file
heuristic produces false-positives; cross-platform CI
attention on tests that use `std::env::temp_dir()`.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
Status update (2026-06-22): Bug â†’ Issue rename closed.

- `199ed91` â†’ closed. Commits `20efe38` (the rename) and
  `6184019` (a regression fix the rename's migration code
  shipped without coverage). 189 workspace tests green
  (was 185).

The rename: types, CLI surface, wire format, on-disk
paths, and bookmark all migrated v1 â†’ v2. Forward-compat
parser reads both `Jjf-Bug:` and `Jjf-Issue:`; writer
emits v2. Spec revved to v2.

The regression: the inline `Storage::open` migration was
unreachable from the CLI (preflight refused before storage
ran) AND broken even when reachable (history walker
filtered post-migration paths only, dropping every
pre-migration op from the chain). Both defects fixed in
`6184019`; a new integration test
`v1_to_v2_migration_preserves_history` synthesizes a v1
repo and exercises the full migration + read path so this
can't recur silently.

Live planner data migrated successfully â€” the 10 issues
on the bookmark formerly known as `bugs` (now `issues`)
are all readable via `jjf ls` and `jjf show`.

Downstream `7100b51` (type + slug fields) is unblocked
and is next.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
Shim flipped (2026-06-22, commit `7371b17`).

`bin/jjf` now delegates to the Rust binary instead of git-bug:
picks the newer of `target/release/jjf` vs `target/debug/jjf`
by mtime so a stale release binary can't shadow a fresh
`cargo build`; builds release on demand if neither exists.

`./bin/jjf ready` and `./bin/jjf ls` smoke-tested. Archived
pre-cutover data is now reached via `git-bug` directly,
per the updated CLAUDE.md.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
Closing as done 2026-06-28.

The "cli-replace-shim" remaining task on the roadmap was a phantom â€”
the shim flip shipped during the 2026-06-22 cutover in commit
`7371b179` ("bin/jjf: delegate to the Rust binary"). The current
`bin/jjf` is a 30-line bash wrapper that picks the newer of
`target/release/jjf` / `target/debug/jjf` (or builds release on
demand) and `exec`s it. The git-bug shell shim is gone.

Kept the wrapper rather than dropping `bin/jjf` entirely: the
newer-of-release-vs-debug picker prevents the "stale release binary
shadows a fresh `cargo build`" footgun, and the build-on-demand
fallback means a fresh checkout's first `./bin/jjf` invocation just
works. Both are real ergonomic wins that a bare `target/release/jjf`
invocation wouldn't carry.

Full verb set is shipping: init / new / show / ls / ready / update /
comment / close / open / block / unblock / abandon / label / dep /
remote / push / pull / remember / memories / recall / forget / assign
/ search / stale, every one with --json. No follow-ups.
